title: Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set at http://demo.testfire.net/sitemap.xml
mitigation: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

llm_triage.justification: The alert is triggered because no CSP header was found, but this is a valid XML file and does not require a CSP header.
remediation_steps: No action required for this alert

title: Missing Anti-clickjacking Header at http://demo.testfire.net
mitigation:
Modern Web browsers support the Content-Security-Policy and X-Frame-Options HTTP headers. Ensure one of them is set on all web pages returned by your site/app.
If you expect the page to be framed only by pages on your server (e.g. it's part of a FRAMESET) then you'll want to use SAMEORIGIN, otherwise if you never expect the page to be framed, you should use DENY. Alternatively consider implementing Content Security Policy's "frame-ancestors" directive.

llm_triage.justification: The absence of an anti-clickjacking header (X-Frame-Options) allows a malicious site to embed the target URL in an iframe, potentially leading to clickjacking attacks.
remediation_steps: Add X-Frame-Options header with value 'SAMEORIGIN' to prevent framing from other domains.

title: Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set at http://demo.testfire.net/robots.txt
mitigation: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

llm_triage.justification: The URL http://demo.testfire.net/robots.txt is a standard location for robots exclusion rules and does not require a Content Security Policy (CSP) header.

title: Cookie without SameSite Attribute at http://demo.testfire.net/sitemap.xml
mitigation: Ensure that the SameSite attribute is set to either 'lax' or ideally 'strict' for all cookies.

llm_triage.justification: The HTTP response headers do not contain a SameSite attribute for the JSESSIONID cookie, which can lead to CSRF vulnerabilities.
remediation_steps: Configure the web application to include the SameSite attribute in the Set-Cookie header for the JSESSIONID cookie. For example: Set-Cookie: JSESSIONID=<session_id>; SameSite=Lax

title: Cookie without SameSite Attribute at http://demo.testfire.net/robots.txt
mitigation: Ensure that the SameSite attribute is set to either 'lax' or ideally 'strict' for all cookies.

llm_triage.justification: The Set-Cookie header is missing the SameSite attribute, which can lead to CSRF attacks.
remediation_steps: Add the SameSite attribute to the JSESSIONID cookie in the web application's configuration files (e.g., web.xml or application.properties).
Set the value of the SameSite attribute to 'Lax' or 'Strict' depending on the application's requirements.
